source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A parallel-plate capacitor is made of circular plates as shown in Fig. 2.10. The voltage across the plates (supplied by long resistanceless lead wires) has the time dependence $V=V_{0} \cos \omega t$. Assume $d \ll a \ll c / \omega$, so that fringing of the electric field and retardation may be ignored. What current flows in the lead wires and what is the current density in the plates as a function of time?
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image placeholder string rather than a file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干显式提到“as shown in Fig. 2.10”，并且输入字段标注 requires_image=true。
text_dominant=false，说明该样本不是纯文本主导。
图像已登记且 registry_passed=true，但题干同时给出了关键几何条件（圆板、d<<a<<c/omega），因此不打满分。
multi_step: 问题要求同时求 lead wires 中的电流和 plates 中的电流密度，至少包含两个相关目标量。
题干给出时变电压 V=V0 cos(omega t) 以及几何近似条件，通常需要结合电容关系、时间求导和电流连续性进行推导。
答案为两个解析表达式（总电流与径向电流密度），表明不是一步直接读数题。
verifiability: answer_type=set，且 answer_text 给出了明确的封闭形式公式，目标答案边界清晰。
结果包含可核验的物理量与变量依赖（I(t) 与 jr(r,t)），便于用符号规则或物理约束检查。
asset_registry_record 中 answer_present=true 且 registry_passed=true，说明样本资产完整。

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A parallel-plate capacitor is made of circular plates as shown in Fig. 2.10. The voltage across the plates (supplied by long resistanceless lead wires) has the time dependence $V=V_{0} \cos \omega t$. Assume $d \ll a \ll c / \omega$, so that fringing of the electric field and retardation may be ignored. What current flows in the lead wires and what is the current density in the plates as a function of time?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image placeholder string rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: Let $\sigma$ be the surface charge density of the upper plate which is the interface between regions I and II. We have $$\sigma=-\varepsilon_{0}E_{z}^{(\mathrm{I})}=\frac{\varepsilon_{0}V_{0}}{d}\cos\omega t.$$ Then the total charge on the plate is $$Q=\pi a^{2}\sigma=\frac{\pi a^{2}\varepsilon_{0}V_{0}}{d}\cos\omega t.$$ Note that $\sigma$ is uniform because $E_{z}^{(1)}$ is uniform for any instant $t$. The time variation of $Q$ shows that an alternating current $I$ passes through the lead wires: $$I=\frac{dQ}{dt}=-\frac{\pi a^{2}\varepsilon_{0}V_{0}\omega}{d}\sin\omega t.$$ As the charge $Q$ on the plate changes continuously with time, there will be surface current flowing in the plate. As shown in Fig. 2.11, this current flows towards the center of the plate along radial directions. The total current flowing through the shaded loop is $$i=-\frac{d}{dt}\left[\pi\left(a^{2}-r^{2}\right)\sigma\right]=\frac{\pi\left(a^{2}-r^{2}\right)\varepsilon_{0}V_{0}\omega}{d}\sin\omega t.$$ Hence, the linear current density (current per unit width) in the plate is $$j_{r}(r)=\frac{i}{2\pi r}\,e_{r}=\frac{\left(a^{2}-r^{2}\right)\varepsilon_{0}V_{0}\omega}{2dr}\sin\omega t\,e_{r}.$$
